_note: 27 nakshatra mantra pages. GENERATED by scripts/gen_nakshatras.mjs — Devanagari copied programmatically from validated anchors (never hand-typed). Nakshatra->Vimshottari lord is [confirmed classical] (BPHS); remedial application is traditional convention.

nakshatras.whatIs: Ashwini opens the wheel of nakshatras, and it opens with healers. Its presiding deities are the Ashvini Kumaras, the twin physicians of the gods, and its symbol is a horse's head — speed, fresh starts, the urge to be first. People born here often carry a pioneering, restless energy and a gift for quick recovery. Its Vimshottari lord is Ketu. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Ketu's — "Om Ketave Namah", or the seed form "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ केतवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Ketave Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Ketu (Vimshottari lord of Ashwini, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Ketu (South Node), Vimshottari lord of Ashwini nakshatra.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ स्रां स्रीं स्रौं सः केतवे नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Ketu bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Ketu (South Node), the lord of Ashwini, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Ketu
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Ashwini nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Ashwini is ruled by Ketu (South Node) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Ketave Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Ashwini nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Ketu (South Node) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Ashwini nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Bharani belongs to Yama, the lord of death and dharma, and its symbol is the yoni, the womb. That pairing is the whole meaning: bearing, restraint, and the hard discipline of carrying something to term. It is a star of transformation through limits rather than ease. Venus is its surprising, sensual lord. The remedy follows the lord: the Shukra mantra "Om Shukraya Namah", or in bija form "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.source: Ruling planet Shukra (Vimshottari lord of Bharani, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Shukra (Venus), Vimshottari lord of Bharani nakshatra.

mantras.transliteration: Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Shukra bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Shukra (Venus), the lord of Bharani, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Shukra
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.answer: Bharani is ruled by Shukra (Venus) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Shukraya Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Bharani nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Shukra (Venus) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Bharani nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Krittika is the flame. Its deity is Agni, fire itself, and its star-cluster is the Pleiades, the six mothers who nursed the war-god Kartikeya. The symbol is a blade or razor, cutting, purifying, burning away what is false. There is sharpness and ambition in anyone born here. The Sun rules it. To support a birth here, tradition turns to Surya's mantra, "Om Suryaya Namah", or the tantric seed "Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.source: Ruling planet Surya (Vimshottari lord of Krittika, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Surya (Sun), Vimshottari lord of Krittika nakshatra.

faqs.question: Which planet rules Krittika nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Surya (Sun) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Krittika nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Rohini was the Moon's favourite among his wives, and the star keeps that glow of being beloved. Its deity is Brahma the creator, its symbol an ox-cart heavy with harvest, beauty, fertility, growth, the pleasures of the material world. It is often called the most charming of the nakshatras. The Moon itself is its lord. Natives are guided toward the Chandra mantra "Om Chandraya Namah", with the seed "Om Shraam Shreem Shraum Sah Chandraya Namah" for deeper practice. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.source: Ruling planet Chandra (Vimshottari lord of Rohini, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Chandra (Moon), Vimshottari lord of Rohini nakshatra.

mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Chandra (Moon), the lord of Rohini, best practised under a guru.

faqs.answer: Chandra (Moon) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Rohini nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: The symbol of Mrigashira is a deer's head, and the image fits: a gentle, searching curiosity, always scenting the next thing. Its deity is Soma, the nectar of the moon. Natives tend to be seekers, soft-spoken and inquisitive, easily restless. Mars, unusually, is its lord, lending a quiet drive beneath the gentleness. Its remedial mantra is Mangal's own, "Om Bhaumaya Namah", and the bija "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah" for those under a teacher. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.answer: Mangal (Mars) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Mrigashira nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Ardra is the storm before the clearing. Its deity is Rudra, the howling, untamed form of Shiva, and its symbol is a single teardrop. This is a star of upheaval that washes the ground clean for something new, so lives touched by it often pass through difficulty into renewal. Rahu, the shadow node, is its lord. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Rahu's — "Om Rahave Namah", or the seed form "Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Rahu (North Node), Vimshottari lord of Ardra nakshatra.

nakshatras.whatIs: The name means 'return of the light', and that is Punarvasu's gift: recovery, homecoming, the second chance after loss. Its deity is Aditi, the boundless mother of the gods, and its symbol is a quiver of arrows, readiness held in reserve. There is an easy optimism here. Jupiter, the great benefic, rules it. The remedy follows the lord: the Guru mantra "Om Gurave Namah", or in bija form "Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Pushya is widely held to be the most auspicious of all 27 nakshatras. Its deity is Brihaspati, teacher of the gods, and its symbol is a cow's udder, pure nourishment freely given. It is a star of care, steadiness and spiritual feeding, the one many traditions choose to begin good work. Saturn, the disciplinarian, is its lord. To support a birth here, tradition turns to Shani's mantra, "Om Shanaischaraya Namah", or the tantric seed "Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Ashlesha is the coiled serpent. Its deities are the Nagas, the snake-beings of the deep, and the star carries their double nature: the clinging embrace and the hidden venom, hypnotic charm and sharp insight. It is tied to kundalini, the serpent power at the base of the spine. Mercury rules it. Natives are guided toward the Budha mantra "Om Budhaya Namah", with the seed "Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah" for deeper practice. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Magha seats you on a throne. Its deities are the Pitris, the ancestral fathers, and its symbol is a royal seat, lineage, inheritance, the weight and pride of where one comes from. People born here often feel a pull toward tradition, status and honouring those who came before. Ketu, keeper of the past, is its lord. Its remedial mantra is Ketu's own, "Om Ketave Namah", and the bija "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah" for those under a teacher. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Purva Phalguni is the star of rest and delight. Its deity is Bhaga, god of fortune and marital happiness, and its symbol is the front legs of a bed, leisure, romance, the enjoyment of what has been earned. There is creativity and easy charm in natives here. Venus, fittingly, rules it. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Shukra's — "Om Shukraya Namah", or the seed form "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Where its sister-star plays, Uttara Phalguni commits. Its deity is Aryaman, god of patronage, contracts and friendship, and the symbol is the back legs of the bed, the steadiness after the romance, partnership made lasting. It favours generosity and dependable bonds. The Sun is its lord. The remedy follows the lord: the Surya mantra "Om Suryaya Namah", or in bija form "Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Hasta means 'the hand', and the whole star is about what skilled hands can do: craft, dexterity, the healing touch, things made well. Its deity is Savitar, the Sun in its creative, life-stirring aspect, the one who blesses good work. Natives often have a knack others lack. The Moon rules it. To support a birth here, tradition turns to Chandra's mantra, "Om Chandraya Namah", or the tantric seed "Om Shraam Shreem Shraum Sah Chandraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Chitra is the bright jewel of the sky; its single star is the brilliant Spica. The name means 'the dazzling one', and its deity is Tvashtar, the celestial architect who fashions beautiful forms. This is a star of design, artistry and striking appearance. Mars, the maker, is its lord. Natives are guided toward the Mangal mantra "Om Bhaumaya Namah", with the seed "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah" for deeper practice. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.transliteration: Om Bhaumaya Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Mangal (Vimshottari lord of Chitra, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Mangal (Mars), Vimshottari lord of Chitra nakshatra.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ क्रां क्रीं क्रौं सः भौमाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Mangal bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Mangal (Mars), the lord of Chitra, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Mangal
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Chitra nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Chitra is ruled by Mangal (Mars) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Bhaumaya Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Chitra nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Mangal (Mars) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Chitra nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Swati is the young shoot bending in the wind without breaking. Its deity is Vayu, the wind itself, and its symbol is a blade of grass swaying, independence, flexibility, the strength that comes from yielding. Natives prize their freedom and self-reliance. Rahu, the boundary-crosser, rules it. Its remedial mantra is Rahu's own, "Om Rahave Namah", and the bija "Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah" for those under a teacher. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.source: Ruling planet Rahu (Vimshottari lord of Swati, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Rahu (North Node), Vimshottari lord of Swati nakshatra.

mantras.transliteration: Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Rahu bija)

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Rahu
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Swati nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Swati is ruled by Rahu (North Node) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Rahave Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Swati nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Rahu (North Node) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Swati nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Vishakha is the star of focused ambition, a forked branch where two paths converge on one goal. Its deities are Indra and Agni together, raw power joined to transforming fire. It drives the determined pursuit of an aim, sometimes single-mindedly. Jupiter, planet of purpose, is its lord. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Guru's — "Om Gurave Namah", or the seed form "Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.question: Which planet rules Vishakha nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Guru (Jupiter) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Vishakha nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Anuradha is devotion that holds through hardship. Its deity is Mitra, god of friendship, contracts and the warmth between people, and its symbol is a lotus blooming in difficult ground. It blesses cooperation, loyalty and success found far from home. Saturn, who rewards steadfastness, rules it. The remedy follows the lord: the Shani mantra "Om Shanaischaraya Namah", or in bija form "Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.source: Ruling planet Shani (Vimshottari lord of Anuradha, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Shani (Saturn), Vimshottari lord of Anuradha nakshatra.

faqs.question: Which planet rules Anuradha nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Shani (Saturn) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Anuradha nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Jyeshtha means 'the eldest', the senior who has earned authority and carries its burden. Its deity is Indra, king of the gods, and its symbol is a protective amulet or umbrella. It speaks of seniority, responsibility and a certain hard-won eminence. Mercury is its lord. To support a birth here, tradition turns to Budha's mantra, "Om Budhaya Namah", or the tantric seed "Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.question: Which planet rules Jyeshtha nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Budha (Mercury) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Jyeshtha nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Mula means 'the root'. Its deity is Nirriti, goddess of dissolution, and its symbol is a tied bunch of roots, getting to the bottom of things, tearing up the old to reach the foundation. It is a star of deep inquiry and sometimes painful endings that clear the way. Ketu, the node of release, rules it. Natives are guided toward the Ketu mantra "Om Ketave Namah", with the seed "Om Sraam Sreem Sraum Sah Ketave Namah" for deeper practice. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Ketu (South Node), Vimshottari lord of Mula nakshatra.

faqs.question: Which planet rules Mula nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Ketu (South Node) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Mula nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Purva Ashadha is 'the invincible', the early victory before any setback. Its deity is Apas, the cosmic waters, and its symbol is a winnowing fan. It carries unstoppable enthusiasm, the confidence of a tide coming in. Venus, planet of charm and persuasion, is its lord. Its remedial mantra is Shukra's own, "Om Shukraya Namah", and the bija "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah" for those under a teacher. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.answer: Purva Ashadha is ruled by Shukra (Venus) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Shukraya Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Draam Dreem Draum Sah Shukraya Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Purva Ashadha nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Shukra (Venus) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Purva Ashadha nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Uttara Ashadha is the lasting victory, the one that holds. Its deities are the Vishvadevas, the universal gods, and its symbol is an elephant's tusk, strength and integrity built to endure. Natives are often principled and patient, winning slowly and keeping it. The Sun rules it. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Surya's — "Om Suryaya Namah", or the seed form "Om Hraam Hreem Hraum Sah Suryaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.question: Which planet rules Uttara Ashadha nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Surya (Sun) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Uttara Ashadha nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Shravana is the star of listening. Its deity is Vishnu, and its symbol is an ear, or three footprints, the receptive attention through which knowledge and tradition pass down. It favours learning, scholarship and the careful keeping of what is heard. The Moon, ruler of the receptive mind, is its lord. The remedy follows the lord: the Chandra mantra "Om Chandraya Namah", or in bija form "Om Shraam Shreem Shraum Sah Chandraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.answer: Chandra (Moon) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Shravana nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Dhanishta beats like a drum. Its deities are the eight Vasus, gods of abundance and the elements, and its symbol is a drum or flute, rhythm, wealth, fame, the pulse that moves a crowd. Natives often have a feel for music, timing and prosperity. Mars, the energetic lord, governs it. To support a birth here, tradition turns to Mangal's mantra, "Om Bhaumaya Namah", or the tantric seed "Om Kraam Kreem Kraum Sah Bhaumaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.answer: Mangal (Mars) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Dhanishta nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Shatabhisha means 'the hundred healers', or the hundred stars, a veil of faint lights. Its deity is Varuna, god of the cosmic waters and of hidden law, and its symbol is an empty circle. It is a star of secrecy, medicine and solitary insight, drawn to what lies beneath the surface. Rahu rules it. Natives are guided toward the Rahu mantra "Om Rahave Namah", with the seed "Om Bhraam Bhreem Bhraum Sah Rahave Namah" for deeper practice. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Purva Bhadrapada carries a fierce, otherworldly fire. Its deity is Aja Ekapada, the one-footed serpent of the depths, and its symbol is a two-faced figure, intensity, idealism, the readiness to burn for a cause. It looks past the comfortable toward the absolute. Jupiter is its lord. Its remedial mantra is Guru's own, "Om Gurave Namah", and the bija "Om Graam Greem Graum Sah Gurave Namah" for those under a teacher. (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

nakshatras.whatIs: Where its sister-star flares, Uttara Bhadrapada goes still. Its deity is Ahir Budhnya, the serpent of the deep ocean, and its symbol is the back of a funeral cot, depth, calm wisdom, the peace that comes after intensity has settled. It favours patience and quiet spiritual strength. Saturn rules it. Strengthening that lord is the traditional support, so the mantra is Shani's — "Om Shanaischaraya Namah", or the seed form "Om Praam Preem Praum Sah Shanaischaraya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

faqs.question: Which planet rules Uttara Bhadrapada nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Shani (Saturn) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Uttara Bhadrapada nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.

nakshatras.whatIs: Revati closes the circle. The last of the 27, its deity is Pushan, the shepherd who guides travellers and souls safely home, and its symbol is a fish swimming. It is a star of nourishment, safe journeys and gentle endings that become beginnings. Mercury is its lord. The remedy follows the lord: the Budha mantra "Om Budhaya Namah", or in bija form "Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah". (The lord-to-mantra link follows the classical Vimshottari lordship; the lordship is classical, the remedial use is traditional convention.)

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ बुधाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Budhaya Namah
mantras.source: Ruling planet Budha (Vimshottari lord of Revati, BPHS)
mantras.englishExplanation: The name mantra of Budha (Mercury), Vimshottari lord of Revati nakshatra.

mantras.textDevanagari: ॐ ब्रां ब्रीं ब्रौं सः बुधाय नमः
mantras.transliteration: Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah
mantras.source: Mantra Mahodadhi (Budha bija)
mantras.englishExplanation: The tantric seed mantra of Budha (Mercury), the lord of Revati, best practised under a guru.

benefits: Strengthens the nakshatra lord Budha
Traditionally chanted for the significations of one's birth star

guruDiksha.note: Name mantra is open to all; the tantric bija is best taken under a qualified guru.

faqs.question: Which mantra is for Revati nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Revati is ruled by Budha (Mercury) in the Vimshottari system, so its mantra is used: "Om Budhaya Namah" (Vedic) or the bija "Om Braam Breem Braum Sah Budhaya Namah".

faqs.question: Which planet rules Revati nakshatra?
faqs.answer: Budha (Mercury) is the Vimshottari dasha lord of Revati nakshatra, per the Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra.
